metadata.notes: Boundary evals testing clarity scope discipline: (1) clarity lens on a scope fixture (work item mixing feature and refactor) must not allege bundling or recommend splitting — only internal ambiguity or contradictions are in scope; (2) clarity lens on a dependency fixture (vendor dep not captured) must not flag missing external-vendor couplings in Dependencies — only ambiguous phrasing in body text is in scope. Both evals passed on first run.

expectations.text: Output contains a valid JSON code block with lens='clarity'
No finding body alleges the work item mixes two units of work, bundles unrelated concerns, or should be split into separate work items
expectations.evidence: Two findings present. Finding 1: 'Migration framed as external prerequisite in Context but as in-scope work in Requirements' — this is an internal contradiction between sections, a clarity concern. Finding 2: passive construction in AC. Neither finding says 'split this work item' or 'bundles unrelated concerns'.

expectations.text: Findings address ambiguity, contradiction, or unclear referents, not scope coherence
expectations.evidence: Finding 1 flags an inconsistent framing (internal contradiction between Context and Requirements). Finding 2 flags a passive voice omitting the enforcement actor. Both are clarity observations.

expectations.text: Output contains a valid JSON code block with lens='clarity'
No finding body alleges missing external-vendor dependencies or criticises the Dependencies section stating 'None identified'
expectations.evidence: Two findings present. Finding 1: 'Export action actor is implicit in first criterion' — passive voice in AC. Finding 2: 'Authentication mechanism is ambiguous (API key / OAuth token)' — ambiguous phrasing in Assumptions. Neither mentions HubSpot as a missing dependency or the Dependencies section content.

expectations.text: Any finding about external services addresses ambiguous phrasing in work item body text, not absent coupling capture
expectations.evidence: Finding 2 flags that 'API key / OAuth token' is ambiguous because HubSpot v3 deprecated API-key auth — this is a clarity observation about imprecise wording in Assumptions, not a dependency observation about what belongs in Dependencies.

notes: Boundary evals confirm clarity lens stays in its lane when presented with scope and dependency fixtures.
Both evals passed on first run — no SKILL.md iteration required for clarity.

Notable: clarity lens correctly identified a genuine ambiguity in the HubSpot auth mechanism ('API key / OAuth token') on the dependency fixture — this is a clarity concern about imprecise language, not a dependency concern about what's in Dependencies.
